pdg_node.informal_desc: Return the number of labeled sequences in dataset_state.

declaration.name: dataset_length_query
declaration.type_signature: (dataset_state: FastaDatasetState) -> int
declaration.docstring: Return the number of labeled sequences in dataset_state.
declaration.conceptual_summary: Labeled Collection Cardinality Extractor. This operation maps a state container to a single scalar by reading the cardinality of a required identifier index within that container. It does not transform, reorder, or inspect element content; it only extracts structural size information from the current state snapshot. The output is an exact non-negative integer equal to the current number of indexed entries.. Applications: Counting annotated waveform segments in a biomedical signal processing registry before batched analysis, Retrieving the number of pending work-unit IDs in a distributed scheduler state for resource allocation, Measuring labeled tile count in a remote-sensing annotation manifest prior to training/evaluation split generation, Querying component-identifier count in a mechanical assembly model to gate downstream simulation complexity

pdg_node.informal_desc: Retrieve a single labeled sequence by index from immutable dataset_state.

declaration.name: dataset_item_retrieval
declaration.type_signature: (dataset_state: FastaDatasetState, idx: int) -> Tuple[str, str]
declaration.docstring: Retrieve a single labeled sequence by index from immutable dataset_state.
declaration.conceptual_summary: aligned_pair_index_lookup. Given an immutable container composed of two positionally aligned 1-D collections, this operation performs direct integer-index selection into both collections at the same coordinate. It returns the paired elements as a 2-tuple, preserving alignment semantics without modifying source state. The transform is a lossless projection from a size-N paired collection to a single pair under a validity constraint on the index.. Applications: Signal processing: retrieve one (channel_id, sampled_trace) pair from aligned channel metadata and waveform arrays., Computer vision: fetch one (annotation_tag, encoded_feature_string) pair from parallel label/descriptor stores., Network engineering: select one (node_identifier, serialized_state_vector) pair from indexed topology snapshots., Manufacturing analytics: access one (part_code, sensor_event_sequence) pair from synchronized production records.

pdg_node.statement: (dataset_state: FastaDatasetState, toks_per_batch: int, extra_toks_per_seq: int) -> List[List[int]]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Compute batch index groups under a token budget using sequence strings and per-sequence overhead.

declaration.name: token_budget_batch_planning
declaration.type_signature: (dataset_state: FastaDatasetState, toks_per_batch: int, extra_toks_per_seq: int) -> List[List[int]]
declaration.docstring: Compute batch index groups under a token budget using sequence strings and per-sequence overhead.
declaration.conceptual_summary: Length-Constrained Sequential Packing. Transforms an ordered collection of variable-size elements into nested index groups under a fixed per-group capacity constraint. Each element contributes a cost equal to its intrinsic length plus a constant per-element overhead. A greedy single-pass rule appends the next element if feasible; otherwise it closes the current group and starts a new one.. Applications: Satellite telemetry framing: pack variable-length sensor records into downlink frames with fixed per-record protocol overhead, Additive manufacturing job staging: group parts into print runs under machine-time limits with per-part setup overhead, Medical imaging pipeline orchestration: bundle scans of varying slice counts into compute batches under memory limits with per-scan preprocessing overhead, Robotics communication scheduling: aggregate variable-size control/state messages into cycle windows with fixed per-message encoding overhead
